destinations.evidence: Async pool treats Scheduled and AsyncSettlement originals as system originals that do not lock senders or participate in sender lock removal.
Block verification recovers Scheduled and AsyncSettlement originals with system senders and uses scheduled caller as payer when needed, preserving system/user distinction.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: async pool sender locks exclude system originals and block verification distinguishes scheduled/settlement origins from user-origin nonce locks.
rationale: The pool and block-verification candidates evolve from sender locking in the async pool to block-level duplicate sender checks, while repeatedly preserving exemptions for scheduled and AsyncSettlement origins.
statement: Async pool insertion and block nonce-lock verification reject duplicate non-scheduled originating senders, skip protocol/system transactions and origin transactions referenced by commitments, and exempt scheduled or AsyncSettlement origins from sender-lock and TransactionId indexing rules.
suggested_falsification_tests: Add async metadata for two user origins from same sender and assert SenderLocked; repeat with Scheduled/AsyncSettlement originals and assert no sender lock.
Block fixture with duplicate user-origin async originals but referenced commitment/system origins exempted.
summary: Async pool and block nonce-lock rules distinguish system and user origins.
test_or_falsification_candidate: AsyncPool sender-lock tests plus block nonce-lock fixture.
title: Async pool and block nonce-lock rules distinguish system and user origins
